item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I really just can't get over the main humps of this SCP.

1) If this road is in a commonly frequented area, how are they possibly keeping containment?

2) What happens if you approach from LA 22? The feeling that I get from the article is that its a "one way" phenomenon.

3) The information in the table only confuses the article. The use of the term "Alien" is poor, as is the term "inhabitants". How are mechanical devices inhabiting anything?

4) I think the article needs to clarify that things found within the anomalous region are immobile. Are mechanical devices introduced also immobile? Why not people then? Sorry if you have a pacemaker I suppose.

For me, it was mostly that I had to re-read the article for clarity. Not because I realized what the twist was, but because it was difficult to understand at all.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: There seem to be quite a few SCPs which transport you to a world where things are a bit different, including SCP-1499 and SCP-860, among some others

item.title: Not to be a dick...
item.content:encoded:
…but there's a minor thing bugging me (and probably only me). The description says the truck is a '67 F-100.

This is the back of a '67 F-100: http://image.classictrucks.com/f/features/1306_1967_ford_f_100/43936348/1967-ford-f-100-rear-three-quarter.jpg

The picture is of a 90's ranger, like this one from '96: http://freepages.misc.rootsweb.ancestry.com/~hoeckelberg/1996-Ranger/RangerRear.jpg

The model year of the truck doesn't seem to affect the rest of the article, so would it be wrong to update the year to match the picture?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
There are some thigns I don't understand.

Previously functional mechanisms do not appear to be functional after crossing into SCP-1855. Objects not functional within SCP-1855 include analog clocks, worm gears, car transmissions, and electric drills. Mechanical systems are impossible to power; objects activated by manually power are impossible to operate without damaging the object.

Well, if this is true, then…

[DATA EXPUNGED] powered clocks do not register as if time were moving forward or backward. There do not appear to be day and night cycles within SCP-1855. Time of day is the same as it was the time SCP-1855 was entered.

How do they know time is frozen? If no clocks could work because the anomaly screws with the mechanics of the clocks, then how do they know that the clocks don't register time?

Alien subjects do not appear to be affected by any otherwise inhospitable ‘natural’ irregularities in SCP-1855’s atmosphere.

I'm not certain what is being talked about here. "Alien subjects" refers to the D-class and agents they send through the thing right? And other than everything being frozen in time, what "Inhospitable natural irregularities" are there in the atmosphere, if it is supposedly just like the normal road?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Don't I know it…our house is right off a very remote Louisiana backroad.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: West Louisiana backroads are horrifying at night.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: As a Louisiana native, I love this…now I know why our van died that one time…

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
The area itself is heavily wooded and hosts residential properties and small dumping areas.

Then wouldn't the Foundation need to somehow get those people living there out of the area? Or maybe the article should say that the SCP is only the road itself, excluding the area surrounding the road?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Uh… I didn't think of that. Probably one of those novelty plates, something like TME GST.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: What about the truck's license plate?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I will work on a clean up when possible, It's just a bit slowed because I don't have access to the internet.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Thanks, edited.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: That's usually a good sign for a downvote, depending on your voting habits of course.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
The truck is designated as -1, but in the interview the driver is called -1. I'd say it would work best to have the driver be -1, but it could also work if you specify that both the truck and driver held the designation collectively.

The whole description in general could use some tweaking to aid clarity, but I'm not certain what the best advice would be. One thing, it might help if instead of naming the normal stretch of road as the main SCP you say that "SCP-1855 is an anomalous zone accessible from (description of road goes here)" That fits better and eliminates the need for a -2 entirely.

"Alien Subjects" made me think it was talking about things that live in the anomaly. But, I have no idea what would be a better phrase to indicate subjects from our world, as opposed to things native to the anomaly.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Liking it, but just one problem. I don't know much about cars but how can one continue driving with a failing transmission?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Shows problems with tone in the description, and the part in the containment procedure about turning civvies away needs to be fixed. Withholding vote for now.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Glad to see this back and better, I really liked this one. The end interview just has a quiet charm.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I love stuff like this. Upvoted.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Alien inhabitants, such as subjects entering SCP-1855-2, do not appear vulnerable to any irregularities or hazards that would otherwise be lethal to them.

The creatures, fires, radio waves, etc are frozen in time and space, but the test subjects we send in are able to move around, breathe, etc.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Overall like the idea but as several have stated some awkward wording needs to be fixed— redundant truck colour, a couple of things that seem overall unnecessary, and a lot of sentences that are just awkward. Also, the distinction between SCP-1855 and SCP-1855-2 is a little confusing. I don't think the article really needs two distinct anomalous areas. I really like the concept though and the ghost truck driver is pretty entertaining. No vote for now.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This seems very clumsy to me, and there are still some issues with wording and style. I wasn't a big fan when this was originally posted and I'm afraid I'm still not. -1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Electrical charge, waves, and oxidation are not present.

How does anyone survive in these zones, if this is the case?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Testing subjects will encounter a white 1967 Ford F-100 pickup truck with white paintjob (SCP-1855-1) upon reaching the 2 km point.

Its redundant to restate the color of the vehicle is redundant. And yes there are Langoliers parallels. Clean up the language a little. No vote.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Definitely better. Need to think on my vote a bit.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Definitely better than the previous posting. Upvoted.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Eh. This improved, but I still find it uninteresting.
Temporal repairs zone, Twilight Zone did it better.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Quite good. Happy to upvote again.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Not impressed, don't hate it though.

I feel that the end could do with a bit more clarity.

The ghost is amusing, as is the mode of entry. I really the description of how the anomaly is entered.
IF cars don't work and you enter by car, does that mean everyone abandons their car and leaves by foot?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: One of the few times that I really like the expungement. Overall this version is more in line with your normal "compellingly confusing" style. I like it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Eh. Well it seems different enough to me to be fine.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: It's the same kind of thing with a world after time has run out. Faminepulse mentioned it in the thread of the previous posting of this.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I don't see the similarity, other than "being transported to another world where things are a bit different."

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: It's still the Langoliers, but it's still pretty good.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I like it, but I'll abstain from voting for now.

"or by means available through current unclassified technology"

The "unclassified" part seems unnecessary.

"SCP-1855 is an area identical to Louisiana Highway 22 and the area around it, continuing on for at least 300 km or indefinitely."

How much of the are around it? If you left the highway and just keep walking/driving away from it what would happen? Would you run into a city? An invisible wall?

"[DATA EXPUNGED] powered clocks do not register as if time were moving forward or backward."

How do we know that this isn't just the clocks not working?

"Things such as freezing temperature or a limited supply of oxygen are extraneous."

Where in this world do you encounter freezing temperatures or a limited supply of oxygen?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Mistyped 3 kilometers as miles.

"Mile" is intentional.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
SCP-1855 is a 3 mile,

Remember we are supposed to use metric.

empty land for about 10 mile in

Mile should be plural.

Over all I find it boring. I'm sorry but -1.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: There's some awkward wording in this one, but I like the idea. No vote for now.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Upvoting for the trucker ghost.

EDIT: Switching from the metric to imperial system is a bad idea, also there are some spacing issues.
